vocabulary.definition: a high, thin voice or sound
vocabulary.example: ” Quirrell laughed, and it wasn’t his usual quivering treble, either, but cold and sharp.

vocabulary.definition: a spell that blocks or reverses another curse
vocabulary.example: I’d have managed it before then if Snape hadn’t been muttering a countercurse, trying to save you.

vocabulary.definition: too curious about other people's business
a substance spread out in the air like mist or steam
vocabulary.example: “Mere shadow and vapor… I have form only when I can share another’s body… but there have always been those willing to let me into their hearts and minds… Unicorn blood has strengthened me,

vocabulary.definition: kindness shown to someone you could punish or harm
vocabulary.example: They died begging me for mercy…

vocabulary.definition: to form painful bubbles on the skin from a burn
vocabulary.example: Quirrell let go of him. The pain in his head lessened—he looked around wildly to see where Quirrell had gone, and saw him hunched in pain, looking at his fingers—they were blistering before his eyes.
Quirrell, full of hatred, greed, and ambition, sharing his soul with Voldemort, could not touch you for this reason. It was agony to touch a person marked by something so good.

vocabulary.definition: to take something away by authority
vocabulary.example: Madam Pomfrey, however, felt it might not be very hygienic, and confiscated it.
Well, they did rather detest each other.

vocabulary.definition: to push or bump against others in a crowd
vocabulary.example: People jostled them as they moved forward toward the gateway back to the Muggle world.

vocabulary.definition: an exciting and often dangerous experience
vocabulary.example: After all, to the well organized mind, death is but the next great adventure.

vocabulary.definition: bravery in facing danger or difficulty
vocabulary.example: There are all kinds of courage,

characters.name: Harry Potter
characters.description: Faces Quirrell and Voldemort alone, then wakes in the hospital wing to learn the truth.

characters.name: Quirinus Quirrell
characters.description: Revealed as the thief trying to steal the Stone for Voldemort.

characters.name: Lord Voldemort
characters.description: Shares Quirrell's body and tries to get the Stone to return to power.

characters.name: Albus Dumbledore
characters.description: Arrives in time to save Harry and explains love, the mirror, and the Stone's fate.

background.hook: The villain in the last chamber is not Snape but Quirrell — with Voldemort hidden beneath his turban. Harry must keep the Stone from them, survive burns and pain, and wake to Dumbledore's answers about love, Snape, and why the Stone was destroyed.
background.readingFocus: Watch how Quirrell and Voldemort each try to get the Stone from the mirror. Notice what Dumbledore explains in the hospital wing and how the house cup is decided at the feast.

options.text: a spell that blocks or reverses another curse
kindness to someone you could harm

comprehension.stem: What is this chapter mainly about?

options.text: Harry confronts Quirrell and Voldemort, survives with Dumbledore's help, and Gryffindor wins the house cup
The trio hatches Norbert and sends him to Charlie
Harry finds the Mirror of Erised in detention
Snape steals the Stone and escapes to London

comprehension.explanation: Quirrell is unmasked as the villain, Harry keeps the Stone through the mirror, Dumbledore destroys it, and last-minute points win Gryffindor the cup.

comprehension.stem: Who really tried to knock Harry off his broom at the Quidditch match?

options.text: Quirrell, while Snape tried to counter the curse
Snape, while Quirrell tried to stop him

comprehension.explanation: Quirrell says he tried to kill Harry and Snape was muttering a countercurse to save him.

comprehension.stem: How does Harry get the Sorcerer's Stone from the mirror?

options.text: He wants to find it but not use it, so the Stone drops into his real pocket
He breaks the mirror with a spell
Quirrell hands it to him as a trick
Hermione solves a riddle on the frame

comprehension.explanation: Dumbledore later explains only someone who wanted to find but not use the Stone could obtain it.

comprehension.stem: Why does Quirrell burn when he touches Harry?

options.text: Harry is protected by his mother's love left in his skin
The Stone burned anyone who lied
Firenze cast a spell on Harry in the forest

comprehension.explanation: Dumbledore says love like Lily's leaves protection in Harry's skin, and it is agony for Quirrell to touch him.

comprehension.stem: What happens to the Sorcerer's Stone at the end?

options.text: Dumbledore destroys it; Nicolas and Perenelle will die after using stored elixir
Harry keeps it in his trunk for next year
Voldemort escapes with it under his cloak
Hagrid hides it in the forbidden forest

comprehension.explanation: Dumbledore says the Stone has been destroyed and Flamel and his wife have enough elixir left to settle their affairs.

comprehension.stem: Why did Snape seem to hate Harry but still protect him?

options.text: James Potter once saved Snape's life, and Snape tried to repay the debt by protecting Harry
McGonagall ordered Snape to favor Gryffindor
Snape wanted Harry to win the house cup bet
Quirrell paid Snape to guard Harry

comprehension.explanation: Dumbledore says Snape could not bear being in James's debt and worked to protect Harry to even the score.

comprehension.stem: How does Gryffindor win the house cup?

options.text: Dumbledore awards last-minute points to Ron, Hermione, Harry, and Neville
They win the final Quidditch match

comprehension.explanation: Ron, Hermione, and Harry earn fifty or sixty points each, and Neville's ten points put Gryffindor ahead of Slytherin.

options.text: a spell that blocks or reverses another curse
a mirror that shows your desires

vocabulary.explanation: Snape muttered a countercurse during the Quidditch match to stop Quirrell's curse.

vocabulary.stem: Quirrell tells Harry he is too ___ to live.
vocabulary.explanation: Quirrell says Harry's snooping on Halloween made him too dangerous to let live.
Dumbledore says it was agony for Quirrell to touch Harry because of Lily's love.

vocabulary.stem: Which word is closest in meaning to 'detest'?
vocabulary.explanation: Dumbledore says Snape and James did rather detest each other at school.

vocabulary.stem: Dumbledore says there are all kinds of ___.
vocabulary.explanation: He awards Neville points for standing up to friends as well as enemies.

cloze.title: The Man with Two Faces
paragraphs: Quirrell, not Snape, wanted the Stone; he laughs without his usual quivering (1) ___. He admits Snape used a (2) ___ to save Harry at Quidditch.
Voldemort shares Quirrell's body like shadow and (3) ___. Harry's touch makes Quirrell's skin (4) ___, because love leaves protection that causes (5) ___ to evil.
Dumbledore destroys the Stone. At the feast he praises many kinds of (6) ___ and awards Neville ten points. On the platform, people (7) ___ Harry as he heads home.

gaps.explanation: Quirrell's laugh is no longer his usual quivering treble.
Snape muttered a countercurse during the broom incident.
Voldemort says he has become mere shadow and vapor.
Quirrell's fingers blister when he grabs Harry.
Dumbledore says it was agony for Quirrell to touch Harry.
Dumbledore says there are all kinds of courage before rewarding Neville.
People jostle them on the way to the gateway at King's Cross.
